# Zinc

> Keyboard-first, security-focused app-sandboxing core. Every user-facing app runs in a
> rootless Podman container (primary) or a libvirt/qemu VM. Priority order: Stable, then
> Secure, then Beautiful.

The 0.1 tools are `zcc` (zinc-container-creator: authors app files, keyboard-first TUI +
CLI) and `zcr` (zinc-container-runner: runs an app file via rootless podman). They share
one library, `common` (the app schema + validation), and meet only at the on-disk YAML
format: zcc authors and shells out to the `zcr` binary to run. The network lock-down is
applied by nftables in the app's own netns before the app starts (fail-closed). Builds are
podman-only: all Go for the tools runs in a digest-pinned container via `make`.

## Docs

- [Architecture (single source of truth)](docs/architecture.md): the full design - schema, security and image trust, the network model, repo layout.
- [README](README.md): overview, components, install, and the podman-only build/run quickstart.
- [Roadmap](ROADMAP.md) and [Releases](RELEASES.md): what is planned and the release plan.

## Reference

- [zcc README](container/creator/README.md): the creator - author/manage app files, the command surface, the zcc/zcr split.
- [zcr README](container/runner/README.md): the runner - run an app file, the fail-closed network model.

## Agent guidance

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): how to work in this repo - golden rules, the runner hexagon, the build gate, and the security model.
- [secops-reviewer agent](.claude/agents/secops-reviewer.md): project-local security reviewer with the Zinc threat model baked in.
